_meta.notes: v1.3.0 — full breed rebalance. Breed feature values are now anchored directly to the real 26-face human MediaPipe calibration (HUMAN_AVG/HUMAN_SD), not a separate hand-estimated breed distribution. Each breed owns exactly one feature+direction as an uncontested 'primary' signature (no other breed competes closely on that axis in that direction); all other features are kept near human-average as light secondary texture. Primary magnitudes are calibrated via target_z = K / scoring_importance (K=2.2) so that every breed has an equal chance of winning the winner-take-most contest when its signature axis is the human's most distinctive feature, regardless of that feature's importance weight. Verified empirically against the 26-face calibration set: all 10 breeds win at least once, no breed exceeds 15% win share (previously French Bulldog 31%, Dachshund 27%, with German Shepherd, Maine Coon, Golden Retriever, Labrador, and Abyssinian winning 0-4% combined). v1.2.0 — pruned to 10 canonical features. jaw_angle remains in the dataset for reference but is excluded from active scoring (scale mismatch between MediaPipe human measurement ~88° avg and prior breed estimates ~46° avg — needs separate recalibration).

schema.name: string — display name
schema.features: object — canonical ID keys, values are numeric | string | null
schema.confidence: object — per-feature confidence flag where relevant
schema.notes: string — breed-specific sourcing or scoring notes

breeds.name: French Bulldog
breeds.notes: Primary signature: shortest nose_length_ratio in the dataset (uncontested) — brachycephalic flat-face archetype. Secondary: moderately wide/round face, wide-set eyes.

breeds.name: Labrador Retriever
breeds.notes: Primary signature: widest jaw_width_ratio in the dataset (uncontested) — sturdy, friendly broad-jaw archetype. Magnitude calibrated higher than other primaries to compensate for jaw_width's lower scoring importance weight. Secondary: warm wide mouth.

breeds.name: Golden Retriever
breeds.notes: Primary signature: widest mouth_width_ratio in the dataset (uncontested) — warm, friendly wide-mouth archetype. Magnitude calibrated higher than other primaries to compensate for mouth_width's lower scoring importance weight. Secondary: kind upward eye tilt, longer snout.

breeds.name: German Shepherd
breeds.notes: Primary signature: narrowest/most elongated face_width_to_height in the dataset (uncontested) — lupine long-face archetype. Secondary: alert eye tilt, longer muzzle.

breeds.name: Dachshund
breeds.notes: Primary signature: longest nose_length_ratio in the dataset (uncontested) — elongated snout archetype. Secondary: narrow face, slightly narrow jaw.

breeds.name: Maine Coon
breeds.notes: Primary signature: largest eye_width_ratio in the dataset (uncontested) — large expressive-eyed archetype. Magnitude calibrated higher than other primaries to compensate for eye_width's lower scoring importance weight. Secondary: broad jaw, moderately round face.

breeds.name: Ragdoll
breeds.notes: Primary signature: only breed with downward eye_tilt (uncontested) — distinctive gentle, sleepy-eyed archetype. Secondary: large round eyes.

breeds.name: Exotic
breeds.notes: Primary signature: roundest face_width_to_height in the dataset (uncontested) — brachycephalic round-face cat archetype. Secondary: flattened nose, larger eyes.

breeds.name: Devon Rex
breeds.notes: Primary signature: most upward eye_tilt in the dataset (uncontested) — distinctive elfin upturned-eye archetype. Secondary: dramatic forehead-to-jaw taper, large eyes.

breeds.name: Abyssinian
breeds.notes: Primary signature: narrowest nose_width_ratio in the dataset (uncontested) — refined, fine-boned archetype. Magnitude calibrated higher than other primaries to compensate for nose_width's lower scoring importance weight. Secondary: alert eye tilt, narrow jaw.
